title: Supreme Commander 2

notes:
Only the top left corner of the display rendered and the game was really slow initially. Pressing anything in the menu took multiple seconds.

As I couldn't change the settings due to this, I had to set my monitor's resolution to the resolution the game defaults to (1024x768). After having done this, I launched the game, turned on windowed mode, then closed the game again, setting my monitor back to 1920x1080, and then launch the game again and set the correct resolution and disabling windowed mode.

After this I played the game for several hours in a single go with no crashes. Noticed some cursor blinking in the main menu but for the rest everything seems to be identical to how it ran on Windows.
title: Supreme Commander 2

title: Supreme Commander 2

notes:
Game renders at 1024x768 on the screen but only on the top left corner. Had to guess where settings were in game to change it to my screen settings (1920x1080) to have it render fine. Other then that, no other noticeable problems.
If similar issue, change your screen settings to 1024x768 then in game set it to your screen settings, leave, change screen settings back, launch. 
Quite sure this could be done on a setting file as well but I don't know where it is.
title: Supreme Commander 2

notes: loaded game from steam and the resolution was incorrect (only partial screen displayed). Had to click around until I found the settings menu (not visible) and was able to change my resolution. Game runs perfect you just have to correct the res issue when you first install.
title: Supreme Commander 2

notes: Main menu (problems with cropped screen, but I managed to bypass them), skirmish (just build a few structures), multiplayer (unplayable due to syncing popup, but all commands were seen by the other player). Game runs fast.
title: Supreme Commander 2

notes:
Same guy as the "Game renders at 1024x768 " bellow. Changed to Manjaro since. Found that proton 4-2-9 causes de-sync issues on mutiplayer. (Message will pop up every time you close it.) and proton 4-11 (newest as time of this review) will not launch the game at all. 3-16 is best so far stable for both offline and online. I also found the setting file since last review for people who have issues with the small rez render. 
/home/(usernamehere)/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/40100/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/Gas Powered Games/Supreme Commander 2/Game.prefs

You want to change the line primary_adapter, which should be in line 600's (exact line changes depending on amount of screens and other small factors)
Would rate it a platinum if it didn't require for some people, manual setting changes and a older proton version.
title: Supreme Commander 2

notes: Works better than the earlier proton versions
